steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage’s conclusion hinges on a conditional relationship: irrigation use increases with concentrated precipitation but is sensitive to the existing baseline. The decisive phrase is “this change in irrigation output is highly sensitive to the baseline concentration of precipitation that currently exists in an area.”

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice B aligns with the conditional logic by showing a slight irrigation increase in the "somewhat concentrated" scenario but a sharper rise (9.0% surface, 7.9% groundwater) when baseline is evenly distributed. The other three either discuss aquifers (A, C) or contradict the prediction of irrigation increasing with concentrated events (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
